item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of my absolute favorites. You did such a great job building on the "potential" dream damage with the stuff that's already happened that my blood legitimately froze. I always love when an article can immerse me fully in its reality like that — these days it takes a lot more than the [insert user name] trick, and your pacing was exquisite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's no canon in the SCP universe; everyone would just be dead otherwise.

What this article implies is lucid dreaming was once just ordinary dreaming. We always had full control, until SCP-4228. Lucid dreaming as we know it is just a pale shadow of that total control. After all, even in lucid dreaming, you can't freely manipulate the dreamscape, just have a modicum of control.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait a moment, does that mean, that in Foundation Universe, lucid dreaming is no longer possible?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This. It appears to rely on a series of popular misconceptions about dreams. It took me a while to realize what it was implying because, well, most of those things still aren't true. It's a bit like saying a particular SCP is the reason we only use ten percent of our brains.

Especially with the very dire descriptions of how intensely contagious the effects are, being spread by merely talking about the show. If Karma Chameleon was as popular as Mickey Mouse, we would barely have a concept of lucid dreaming, much less reams of how-to manuals. I had trouble recognizing the twist until I got to nightmares, and even so, I lost out on the atmospheric suspense I probably should've felt.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this was really great, at first it was reminding me a bit of that one kirby fanfic scp, but the use of dreams and stuff like that helped make it more original, i also like the metaphors the episodes are about, overall, a really creative and fun scp to think about! good job!

1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ahh! I'm so glad you picked up on that!

A lot of people have commented that the disturbing visual imagery feels tacked on, or too reminiscent of the "Bobbles" SCP. I was worried I'd failed at providing a strong enough connection to the reader:

SCP-4228 is alive, the show itself is alive, and the "torture" that Oliver Brothers Inc. did to it affected the episodes themselves.

"Karma Kameleon" is a representation of the memette.
Oliver Brothers Inc. are the "Hollow Men".
The "Sad Machines" are the tools they used to torture the memette.

The Rainbow Forest represents the original purpose of the memette, to make people care about the environment. In the final episode, Karma being removed from that forest and placed in a cage, represents the memette being removed from it's original purpose, and forced to do as the "hollow men", as Oliver Brothers Inc. wanted.

Thanks so much for the read!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had a similar thought - but I think that's part of what makes this a good piece. I saw some of these 'effects of episodes 1-7', and thought to myself, "I must not have seen that episode when it aired".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"There is no music throughout; the majority of sound during this episode is Karma's own panicked breathing."

At that line I was already sold and I didn't even get into the real meat of the scp. As a lover of ecology and zoology that point made me adore the idea of this scp as one that not only gives chilling thoughts about our own world, but also gives some evidence to ideas it brings in like that the scp really is alive.

Solid +1, one of, if not, the best scp I've come across so far and it will be for a while.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's a totally fair. As in all things, I could only base this article on my own experiences, and research. I tried to divide the dream impairments best I could by averages: most people don't smell in their dreams, most have experienced at least one nightmare, etc. The core premise was derived from my long-standing fascination with dreaming in black and white. Despite modern theories that this phenomenon occured due to black-and-white television watching, many young people who've consumed nothing but colour media their entire lives still experience it. Myself included, sometimes, and the fact no one knows why frightens and excites me.

I never hoped to speak to everyone's unique dream experiences, no more than I'd hope to write a coming-of-age tale that captures everyone's childhood perfectly. I regret that this article didn't hit home for you. It's my second in so many months, and I'm working hard to improve. Thanks for the read!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
People have wildly different subjective experiences with regards to imagination and dreaming. I would not be surprised if there are some people who can smell in their dreams. Conversely, some of us don't dream at all, or have greatly reduced senses. I don't really see or hear in my dreams. It's hard to describe exactly how I do experience dreams; the best I can say is that I just know what's going on.

Anyways this SCP really failed for me. I have no idea what dream modifiers are "normal" and which are spooky-anomalous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought you were going here for everyone being caught in a fugue where they'll never know if they are dreaming or not, perhaps to the extent that the only place free from Karma Kameleon is the dream reality we live in. This is more mild, but hits far harder.

item.title: Re: +100!
item.content:encoded: Thanks aismallard :) Here's hoping my future articles are also well received!
Congrats, T Rutherford on another great article!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the read! I always love hearing when different readers get hit by that realization. For some, it's from actual episode 1: "Wait a minute, you can't smell anything when you dr- ooooooh." For others, it's not until the end of the article. It's fascinating how different people read, either analyzing as they go, or reading everything completely, then letting their mind process.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Subjects are no longer aware when/if they are dreaming."

Aaaaand upvote.

For me, that one line was really the string that drew the quietest, but deepest level of horror into the narrative. The more you tug on the idea of a company putting *that* out there, the worse the use cases become.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, given that it's an extremely infectious idea, my take is just that new humans, upon being born, are almost immediately infected by the meme from their parents or hospital staff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

"SCP-4228-EN" is just a designation for the operation of removing "Karma Kameleon" from the world.

You could just call it the "SCP-4228 recontainment operation" or even just "containment operations".

Tried to keep it consistent, but when I added the space it beefed up the second section too much.

I know you can add CSS styling on tables to fix their width as a constant %, but I'm not sure how you do that in Wikidot. Maybe ask in #site17?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Oliver Brothers Inc."? My name is Oliver and i have a Brother! (Yes, i do realize that thats nowhere near as exciting as i think it is.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the read! And the Foundation pulling the plug on dreams entirely? I'd actually enjoy reading that! If you ever end up writing it, let me know.

And yeah. I try, I REALLY do, but I can't seem to write an article without sneaking in some farce.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks so much! I'm glad you enjoyed reading it.

Also, I'm glad I did Marshall, Carter and Dark justice as a "character".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had an idea a while ago about a skip that required the foundation to just pull the plug on dreams entirely, but this handles it way better.

I have it on good authority that Carter alone refuses to light his cigar with anything other than an alt-dimensional duplicate of the American Declaration of Independence!

I realize this is a pretty serious article, but I had to stop for a few minutes to just laugh at this line. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really enjoyed this. A media company using a infohazardous cartoon to destroy imagination so that is has a monopoly on whimsy? Awesome idea and well presented. Well done!

Also:

"I have it on good authority that Carter alone refuses to light his cigar with anything other than an alt-dimensional duplicate of the American Declaration of Independence!"

That's Ruprecht alright.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the read!

Corrected on both counts. Good eye!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That was great well done! +1

couple simple things
see, nothing too major

SCP-4228 follows the adventures a cartoon chameleon “Karma”.

Follows the adventures "of" a cartoon

The (sic) in the summaries aren't always consistent, being inside or outside the quotation marks. Not sure which side you want em but probably should have them all be the same.

Really well done enjoyed it greatly!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good question! The idea was that the minds of humanity were physically altered beyond repair. It's literally how our brains work now. So yes, it's genetic. Perhaps in centuries?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really enjoyed this, a good use of escalation. One question, is the memetic infection acquirable hereditarily or something? How come kids born after the Foundation wiped Karma Chameleon from history haven't "regained" humanity's lost dreaming capabilities?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks so much!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the read!

Response! 
Lotsa Response

These are inconsistent. I would just drop the -EX, they used ENNUI, but part of the scip is still considered anomalous.

I think you're right about the -EX. While half the episodes are no longer anomalous, and technically "explained" by being in effect globally, half of them are not. Keter, however, I'll argue. Containing the effects of episodes 1-7 is impossible, because it's already out. Given the virulence of SCP-4228, I'd say it's warranted.

What does this mean?

"SCP-4228-EN" is just a designation for the operation of removing "Karma Kameleon" from the world. Everything else I came up with felt cliche. If you have ideas, lay em on me!

There are two columns in the middle, where the first table only has one.

A product of previous versions. Removed. I wish I could make the table columns consistent, though.

Also the table styling for the normal and O5-4 versions are inconsistent.

Tried to keep it consistent, but when I added the space it beefed up the second section too much. I like keeping them as line-breaks, and the inconsistency isn't too jarring.

Totally optional obviously, but this might be a good place to hotlink to SCP-4224.

I try not to cross-link too heavily, but I think the one link to SCP-8900-EX is enough. It's my favorite example of the Ennui protocol.

Seriously though thanks for the read, and the crit!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I usually add a collapsible at the bottom of my drafts with a list of critiquers / tags / things I want to mention in the forum post.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Subtley disturbing and atmospheric. +1

+ show block
– hide block

Item #: SCP-4228-EX

Object Class: Keter

These are inconsistent. I would just drop the -EX, they used ENNUI, but part of the scip is still considered anomalous.

Also I think that just keeping it Safe could be interesting, it sort of plays with the fact that "Safe" doesn't mean "harmless". If you still want to feel like the escalation is needed, you could add an escalating threat level, though not all readers like that. Your call.

SCP-4228-EN

What does this mean?

[DATA EXPUNGED]
[DATA EXPUNGED]

There are two columns in the middle, where the first table only has one.

Also the table styling for the normal and O5-4 versions are inconsistent.

Our only option was the Ennui protocol.

Totally optional obviously, but this might be a good place to hotlink to SCP-4224.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of the best articles I've read in a while! You managed to make the kids show really creepy in a Channel Zero kind of way. The whole story with O4 and the implications of it are awesome. Loved it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! I lost my document with all the people who did a draft review. Credited!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very glad I got the chance to see this in the crit stage — it's great!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm so glad the narrative unfolded the way I'd hoped. Thanks for the read!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Damn you did it again. The base anomaly has such a nice creepypasta tone. And then when it starts to unravel, when you notice why it ends up in a boring, still room and especially the implication that human dreams were once… so much greater and more are truly frightening and tragic.

+1000000

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks so much! I really appreciate it :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is awesome. I’m a big fan of video/online anomalies, So naturally, when I read “unaired kids show” (or something like that) there was no question; I was going to read the whole thing. And I did. And I loved it. The thought of our dreams being manipulated is frightening. Back in my not Ao good days, dreaming was my only escape, and having that infected would be terrifying, and really for anyone. Plus just the way you get this across, it’s awespme. Aaaa sorry this is just so cool. +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh wow, how'd I miss that? 13 was the original number of episodes, but I added in an extra to help the flow of narrative. Leave it to me to forget to update the clinical info. Thanks for catching that!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

It says that there are 13 tapes in the series, but there are fourteen listed in the article.

item.title: Karma Kameleon
item.content:encoded:
I like black and white
Dream in black in white
You like black and white
Run runaway

Spoilers Abound, Come Come Around!
Spoiled for the Day, Run Run Away!

SCP-4229 is alive, a memette. The episodes were originally wholesome, and cheerful, but when Karma Kameleon was re-sold to Oliver Brothers Inc. to turn it into an imagination-killer, the content of the episodes began to change. The "Hollow Men" are Oliver Brothers Inc., the "Sad Machines" are the tools they used to destroy the "Rainbow Forest", representing the memette's original purpose. Karma Kameleon, the character, is a visual representation of the memette, and the final episode, Karma taken from the forest and put in a cage, represents the results of it's torture.

The "dream damage", half of which affects the human population to this day, was just a side-effect of this whole affair. If every episode had made it to air? Well, 05-4 describes it best.

Props to greytoiletpaper for first commenting on this!

My second article here, after the truly wonderful reception I received for Stalled Conversation. I hope you enjoy reading it!

Image was taken and edited personally.

Big thanks to N_Aepic_Fael for their in-depth crit! Also Crashington for catching some errors! rattles for a review, If we traded drafts, please let me know!

► More T Rutherford Articles and Tales ◄
